_meta.title: Legacy Trace Prototype — Past-Captain Trail Mechanic
_meta.description: Single beat demonstrating the legacy-trace mechanic codified in MAP.md. A legacy-trace is a faint message left by a past captain on the map — visible to future captains who arrive at the same node. This beat is the discovery moment: the new captain finds the trace and decides what to do with it.
_meta.spec_note: This beat is intentionally minimal — one entry point, two choices, one delta path. It exists so the legacy_trace_system.gd (Phase 3a) has a concrete shape to implement against. Full trace generation, pin placement on the map, and multi-trace discovery chains are out of scope for this beat.

data_spec.note: The fields below describe what legacy_trace_system.gd needs to pass through when this beat fires. They are NOT in the beat itself — they are the contract between the map system and the narrative layer.

legacy_state.last_known_node: string — hex coord or station ID where the trace was left
legacy_state.parting_thoughts: string — the text shown to the discovering captain
legacy_state.captain_n: int — the captain who left this trace

discovering_captain.captain_n: int — the current captain finding this trace

legacy_trace_healing_1.prose: The pin is faint — a ghost-mark on the hex, left by a captain who didn't make it out. You dock. The hull is cold. On the wall, scrawled in marker: "If you're reading this, the Trust lied about the He-3. The clearance code is 7-7-ALPHA. Use it or don't. I'm not coming back." The handwriting is steady. The captain who wrote this was calm when they left.

choices.text: Honor them. Use the clearance code.
Leave it. Carry the weight another day.
